title: Profinite rigidity and flexibility for compact 3-manifold groups
speaker: Alan Reid
affiliation: University of Texas, Austin; Member, School of Mathematics
abstract: This talk will discuss the question: To what extent are the fundamental groups of compact 3-manifolds determined (amongst the fundamental groups of compact 3-manifolds) by their finite quotients. We will discuss work that provides a positive answer for fundamental groups of hyperbolic 1-punctured torus bundles.

proof_stages.label: Finite-quotient genus and profinite dictionary
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets up residually finite groups, the finite-quotient set C(gamma), the genus of a group, and later identifies equality of finite quotients with isomorphism of profinite completions.

proof_stages.label: Rigidity-flexibility landscape and scope
proof_stages.summary: Known positive and negative cases for abelian, nilpotent, lattice, free, and 3-manifold groups motivate restricting the main question to finite-volume hyperbolic 3-manifold groups among compact 3-manifold groups.

proof_stages.label: Target theorem for punctured-torus bundles
proof_stages.summary: The central theorem is stated: a hyperbolic once-punctured-torus bundle over the circle is determined by its finite quotients among compact 3-manifold groups.

proof_stages.label: Topological reduction of the competitor manifold
proof_stages.summary: Given a compact 3-manifold N with the same finite quotients as the target, deep 3-manifold and profinite inputs reduce N to an irreducible compact manifold with a single incompressible torus boundary component, excluding closed and connected-sum alternatives.

proof_stages.label: Homological and finite monodromy reduction
proof_stages.summary: Finite quotients preserve first homology in the relevant covers, hyperbolic punctured-torus monodromies are characterized by all cyclic covers having first Betti number one, and the torsion formula |tr(phi)-2| leaves only finitely many SL_2(Z) monodromy possibilities.

proof_stages.label: Profinite detection of fibering and the F2 fiber
proof_stages.summary: The proof next shows that N is fibered, then uses exactness of the profinite completion sequence and the uniqueness of maps to z-hat to identify the fiber kernel with the profinite completion of F_2, forcing a once-punctured-torus fiber; the non-fibered case uses Wise-style separability machinery.

proof_stages.label: Congruence and omnipotence separation of monodromies
proof_stages.summary: The remaining finitely many monodromies are separated by the congruence subgroup property for Out(F_2), formulated using characteristic finite-index subgroups of F_2, together with omnipotence to force different monodromy orders in a finite quotient.
